rulings.Case Title: IN RE: THE JACQUELINE WARD 2007 REVOCABLE TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #5, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 2/1/22. If a person is absent from the county where his or her attorney's office is located, or for some other cause is unable to sign or verify a pleading, the attorney may sign or verify it, unless the person is, or is seeking to become, a fiduciary appointed in the proceeding. [CRC 7.103(c).] If a petitioner, objector, or respondent is absent from the county or for some other cause is unable to sign or verify a petition, objection, or response filed pursuant to this code, the persons attorney may sign or verify the petition, objection, or response unless the person is a fiduciary appointed in the proceeding. [Probate Code Sect. 1023.]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF TAI YUET CHAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 1/26/22 for a likely appearance. Issue(s) necessitating continuance: No waiver of account has been filed by the other beneficiariy, and it is unclear which terms of the will waived an accounting by the executor. The Corrected I&A did not, in fact, show assets as of the date of death, and Exhibit B to the petition did not show any sums distributed to this executor. Notice to the FTB was only just given and not later than 90 days after letters were issued to the executor on 4/11/2017, as required by Probate Code Section 9202(c). The calculation of the statutory fee would not have been acceptable had the full amount been requested, as no detailed schedules of receipts and gains and losses were included, as required by CRC 7.550(b)(6) and 7.705(b). Without a correct "Corrected I&A" appraised by the Probate Referee, the purported I&A value may need to be supported. (Item 4 of an I&A should NOT be answered when a Probate Referee HAS BEEN appointed.) Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 1/18/22. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE ABDUL AZIZ KHAN AND HAJRA NIGHAT KHAN JOINT LIVING TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Confirming Assets Of Sub Trusts
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. 1) Hajra Khan held a 50% interest in the real property at 291 Mangels Avenue, San Francisco, California, as her sole and separate property, and transferred the same to the 1995 trust. Shortly after Abdul Khan's death, Hajra Khan, with the assistance of her attorney, executed and recorded a deed transferring the 50% interest in the Mangels property to the Exemption Trust, even though the surviving settlor/spouse's separate property was to fund the Survivor's Trust under the terms of the 2010 trust amendment. The petition requests an order confirming the allocation of Hajra Khan's 50% separate property interest in the Mangels property to the Exemption Trust. 2) Hajra Khan held the real property at 811 Seneca Drive, South Lake Tahoe, California, as her sole and separate property. In 1995, shortly after creating the trust, Abdul Khan and Hajra Khan executed and recorded a deed transferring the Seneca property, "as to community property", to themselves as trustees of the 1995 trust. The petition requests an order confirming that Hajra Khan's separate property interest in the Seneca property is an asset of the 1995 trust and is allocable to the Survivor's Trust. The 1995 deed has not been reformed to remove the claimed error of the "community property" description. No petition has been filed under Probate Code sections 850(a)(3)(B) or 2580(a)(6). PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appear by video and that they input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. Please review the Court's 10th Amended General Order which took effect on 06/28/2021 for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JAMES WON LING BONG
rulings.Calendar Matter: PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing; however, the time to administer the estate will only be extended to one year from the date the petition was filed. Unless accounting is waived by all parties, an accounting will be required by the administrator, and this Court limits the first accounting to just two years. [LRSF 14.34 A.] A new status for closing the estate will be set for 10/11/22. Either a petition for final distribution or a second report of status with a petition for additional time for administration that incorporates the accounting for the first two years beginning with the date of death must be filed by no later than 9/27/22.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ANTON BAJJALIEH
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Trial
rulings.Rulings: Hearing appearance required. Remote appearance by video or telephone should be made whenever possible. Please review the 10th amended general order for more information. To appear by Zoom, go to Zoom.com and click Join Meeting, and use the ID and password below. Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Parties appearing should check in for the hearing using their first and last name and case number. ISSUES (may be addressed at hearing): 1) Court to determine how to proceed. 2) Issues in Michelle Bajjalieh Petition of Lost Will: [Same issues since 4/21/2021.] a. A proposed nonresident personal representative will be required to post a bond to protect California creditors, even if the will waives, or all beneficiaries waive, bond. A declaration or attachment to the petition setting forth in detail the anticipated liabilities of the decedent and claims against the estate will be used by the Court to determine the amount of the bond, but in no event will the bond be less than $20,000. [LRSF 14.16 F.] In response attorney E. Neal McGettigan filed a Declaration on 05/10/2021 that is not signed or verified by Petitioner Michelle Bajjalieh. [See CRC 7.103] b. Petitioner Michelle Bajjalieh has not submitted a hard copy of the proposed order to the Examiner in room 202 at least five (5) court days in advance of the scheduled hearing date, with the scheduled hearing date noted on the face sheet. [See LRSF 14.7]. 3) Issues in First Amended Petition for Letters of Administration [Filed 08/11/2021 by Jacqueline Ahwal] a. DE-121 Notice was not given 15 days prior to the hearing to the following individuals directly but was given 1 day prior to the 10/6/2021 hearing: Michelle Bajjalieh, Matthew Joseph Bajjalieh, Terah Bajjalieh, and Debra Dolch.

Petitioner Michelle Bajjalieh has not submitted a hard copy of the proposed order to the Examiner in room 202 at least five (5) court days in advance of the scheduled hearing date, with the scheduled hearing date noted on the face sheet. [See LRSF 14.7]. 3) Issues in First Amended Petition for Letters of Administration [Filed 08/11/2021 by Jacqueline Ahwal] a. DE-121 Notice was not given 15 days prior to the hearing to the following individuals directly but was given 1 day prior to the 10/6/2021 hearing: Michelle Bajjalieh, Matthew Joseph Bajjalieh, Terah Bajjalieh, and Debra Dolch.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE WOON FUNG CHAN REVOCABLE TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Instructions And Confirming Trust Asset
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 2/22/22. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: Due most likely to the dearth of Court staff, the "Second Amendment to Petition for Instructions" (which is, in fact, a fully amended petition, not just a partial amendment to either the original petition that was filed on 6/10/21 or the amended petition that was filed on 8/18/21 and, therefore, should have been captioned "Second Amended Petition Confirming Trust Asset" in order to be accurate) was not E-filed until 12/15/21. Furthermore, notice of this pleading was reportedly mailed on 12/7/21, BEFORE the document was even file-stamped (on 12/8/21) and before it was actually E-filed and available for viewing (on 12/15/21), so notice was defective. [LRSF 14.14 A.] Who are the settlor's heirs-at-law? Was she survived by just one sister and no other sibling? Are the nephew and niece identified in the petition the children of that one sister? Heirs who are not trust beneficiaries are entitled to personal service of notice. A supplement to the "Second Amendment to Petition for Instructions and Confirming Trust Asset", signed by all three co-trustees and verified by at least one of them, must be filed to fully disclose all of the settlor's heirs or to verify (confirm) that (1) she was survived by just the one sister who is named as a trust beneficiary and no other sibling or issue of any predeceased sibling, and (2) she had no issue of her own. A copy of the supplement and of the "Second Amendment to Petition for Instructions and Confirming Trust Asset" must be served on all interested parties after the supplement is actually E-filed and available for viewing. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner BEFORE 2/14/22. It is also noted that the settlor's interest in the real property at issue appears to be 50% interest in 542-546 26th Avenue, not "542 26th Avenue", according to her original trust and the two deeds. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE WILLIAM AND SONIA MARTIN INTER VIVOS TRUST, DATED OCTOBER 19, 2000
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Confirming Certain Assets Are Trust Property (Probate Code Section 850 (A)(3)(B)
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing, although the incorrect form of notice for the initial hearing had been mailed a second time to Helene Fehler at an address in WA, but the correct Notice of Hearing shows that she was served notice at the address set forth in the petition only, and the correct Notice of Hearing was mailed to "Laura" Booth, when "Laurie" Booth is listed in the petition and received (the incorrect form of) notice of the iniital hearing. The "petitioner" should have been shown on the face of the Notice of Hearing (item 1) as the successor trustee, not just "Petitioner", as she is acting in that representative, fiduciary capacity.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE MARILYN LEAL FAMILY TRUST, DATED JUNE 20, 1995 as Amended and Restated thereafter
rulings.Calendar Matter: Verified Probate Section 850 Petition To Recover Funds Distributed From Trust To Beneficiaries Without First Satisfying Assignment Lien, And
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 02/22/2022. Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. 1. Endorsed-filed single sided courtesy copies are required for any papers filed with the court and must be received by Examiner in room 202 at the Superior Court. Examiners will not review any document until after it has been filed and a courtesy copy has been received. [LRSF 2.7, 14.7]. Courtesy copies dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the envelope or a cover page, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. 2. Petitioner must submit a hard copy of the proposed order to the Examiner in room 202 at least five (5) court days in advance of the scheduled hearing date, with the scheduled hearing date noted on the face sheet. [LRSF 14.7]. Proposed orders dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the envelope or a cover page, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. 3. Must give 30 day's notice on the mandatory judicial council form DE-115. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 2/7/2022 [LRSF 14.7 B.] Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF AARON LEVINE
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 2/1/22. No proof of publication has been filed yet. The declination attached to the petition is Fay Yeager's declination to serve as successor trustee, not as co-executor. (The will identifies the sister as Edith Levene, but item 8 of the petition names Edith Levine. Item 8 of the petition should have clearly identified the trustee of the devisee trust; however, the declaration attached to the petition did identify the trustee. The receipts and 13100 affidavit referenced in that declaration, however, have not yet been filed as of 1/7/22.) Courtesy copies of the proof of publication and any other papers filed after 12/15/21 must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 1/21/22. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF NONG JUE LEE STEIN
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 02/08/2022. Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. 1) Petitioners did not include who the Trustee of the Nong Jue Lee Stein Revocable Trust is on page 4 #8. (See petition page 3 #8 all persons mentioned in decedent's will). 2) Petitioners must conventionally file the original will. [See LRSF 14.59 C.] 3) Petitioners must file mandatory judicial council form DE-121. 4) Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Judicial Council Form DE-147S) is also required, pursuant to Probate Code 8404(b). Form DE-147S must be filed separately and confidentially. Do not file form DE-147S as an attachment to the Duties and Liabilities form.[LRSF 14.18 E.] 5) Petitioner must submit a hard copy of the proposed order to the Examiner in room 202 at least five (5) court days in advance of the scheduled hearing date, with the scheduled hearing date noted on the face sheet. [LRSF 14.7]. Proposed orders dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the envelope or a cover page, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 1/24/2022 [LRSF 14.7 B.] Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

(See LRSF 14.20) 2) Duties and Liabilities must include petitioner's phone number see page 2 #2. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 1/31/2022 [LRSF 14.7 B.] Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.
